bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am targeting my maximum utility bid. Since there is no time pressure at t=0.0, I should start firm on my preferred values across all issues and wait for the opponent’s response.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my opening offer. The opponent’s only offer was all valueA, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue and does not yet show meaningful concession, so I will hold firm especially on the high-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04, and the opponent has only made a small concession from all valueA to issueE:valueB. Their offers are improving slightly for me but remain far below my prior offers, so I will concede only on low-weight issues while holding firm on the important issueD and issueE.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.94 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only improved issueE so far while keeping the other issues at their worst values for me, so I will concede slightly on low-weight issuesA and B while holding firm on the more important issuesC, D, and E.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to concede on my most important issues yet. The opponent has improved their offers to me from 0.20 to about 0.38, but their concessions are not cumulative and they still demand very low values on issueA, issueB, and issueC, so I will concede mainly on low-weight issueA/issueB while holding issueC, issueD, and issueE at my best values.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 while making a small concession on a low-weight issue rather than weakening issueD/issueE. The opponent’s offers are mostly very low for me, with only inconsistent concessions on issueD/issueE, so I will not chase their latest bid but will continue conceding first on issueA/issueB.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.88 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me, with only limited movement on issueD/issueE and a recent small move on issueA, so I am conceding mainly on the low-weight issues issueA and issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC, issueD, and issueE.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions on my lowest-weight issues. The opponent’s latest valid offer improved sharply for me by giving issueE: valueE, but they are still keeping issueC low; I will hold firm on the high-weight issueD/issueE and medium issueC while conceding issueA/issueB.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.80 since it is still early (t=0.16) and there is no need to concede to the opponent's latest 0.56-utility offer yet. The opponent is conceding unevenly across high-weight issues, especially showing willingness on issueD and issueC, so I am holding firm on the most important issues D/E while making the main concession on lower-weight A/B and a moderate concession on C to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a high but slightly more flexible utility around 0.76–0.78 because it is still early at t=0.18, so I should not rush down to the opponent’s much lower offers. The opponent has shown some willingness to move on high-weight issues like issueD and issueE, but their offers are inconsistent and still below what I should accept, so I will hold issueD and issueE at valueE while conceding further on lower/mid issues to avoid repeating my last bid.
